The details of this prophecy are scattered and mixed together, and the same things are left and returned to again. Babylon is full of treasures, yet neither her waters nor her wealth will protect her. Destruction comes when they do not expect it. Wherever we are, even in the deepest places or at great distances, we must remember the Lord our God; and during times of greatest fear and hope, it is most important to remember the Lord. The feeling caused by Babylon's fall is the same as that concerning New Testament Babylon. The ruin of all who support idolatry, disbelief, and superstition is necessary for the renewal of true devotion; and the threatening prophecies of Scripture give comfort when viewed in this way. The main center of anti-Christian tyranny, idolatry, and superstition, which persecutes true Christians, is as certainly doomed to destruction as ancient Babylon. Then many people will mourn for sin and seek the Lord. Then the lost sheep of the house of Israel will be brought back to the fold of the good Shepherd and stray no more. And the precise fulfillment of these old prophecies encourages us to have faith in all the promises and prophecies of the sacred Scriptures.

This prophecy is sent to Babylon, to the captives there, by Seraiah, who is to read it to his people in captivity. Let them with faith see the end of these threatening powers and comfort themselves with this. When we see what this world is like, how attractive its appearances are, and how pleasing its offers, let us read in the book of the Lord that it will soon be desolate. The book must be thrown into the river Euphrates. The fall of New Testament Babylon is shown this way. Those who sink under the weight of God’s wrath and curse sink forever. Babylon, and every antichrist, will quickly sink and rise no more forever. Let us hope in God's word and quietly wait for his salvation; then we will see, but will not share, the destruction of the wicked.
